candidates.name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
candidates.bio: Deborah Wasserman Schultz (née Wasserman; /ˈwɑːsərmən/; born September 27, 1966) is an American politician serving as the U.S. representative for Florida's 25th congressional district, first elected to Congress in 2004. A member of the Democratic Party, she was the chair of the Democratic National Committee (DNC) from 2011 until her resignation in 2016.
key_messages: Wasserman Schultz is a proponent of gun control legislation and co-sponsored the STOP School Violence Act to strengthen school safety through training programs and security improvements.
Wasserman Schultz is a strong supporter of Israel, backing its military actions in Gaza and supporting financial aid to Israel, while also supporting the Iran Nuclear Deal.
Wasserman Schultz supports expanding hate crime protections and increasing penalties for identity theft, while advocating against making military veterans a separate category of hate crime victims.
Wasserman Schultz voted in favor of the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act in 2008 during the financial crisis, but opposed CFPB payday lending regulation delays.

campaign_themes.heading: guns
campaign_themes.text: Wasserman Schultz is a proponent of gun control legislation and co-sponsored the STOP School Violence Act to strengthen school safety through training programs and security improvements.

campaign_themes.text: Wasserman Schultz voted in favor of the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act in 2008 during the financial crisis, but opposed CFPB payday lending regulation delays.

platform_excerpts.excerpt: Wasserman Schultz is a proponent of gun control legislation... co-sponsored a bill to 'strengthen school safety and security'... would 'provide $50 million a year for a new federal grant program to train students, teachers and law enforcement on how to spot and report signs of gun violence'.

Wasserman Schultz is a supporter of Israel... has continued to defend the state's military action in the Gaza Strip... In 2015, Rep. Wasserman Schultz announced her support for the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA), commonly known as the Iran Nuclear Deal.

Wasserman Schultz introduced the Stopping Tax Offenders and Prosecuting Identity Theft Act of 2013... would increase the penalties on identity thieves... She remarked that suggesting military members have 'systematically been the victims of hate crimes' is belittling to actual protected groups.

On September 29, 2008, Wasserman Schultz voted for the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008... She was among a dozen Florida representatives who cosponsored the legislation that would delay the CFPB's payday lending rules by two years... She has drawn criticism for trying to delay those regulations.

top_stances.summary: Support gun control legislation and school safety measures through training and security improvements.
top_stances.source_excerpt: co-sponsored the STOP School Violence Act to strengthen school safety through training programs and security improvements

top_stances.summary: Strong supporter of Israel, its military actions, financial aid, and the Iran Nuclear Deal.
top_stances.source_excerpt: strong supporter of Israel, backing its military actions in Gaza and supporting financial aid to Israel, while also supporting the Iran Nuclear Deal
top_stances.track_record_note: Voted YEA on s4465-119 (FISA Amendments Act extension) aligning with foreign policy engagement; voted YEA on hconres75-119 directing removal of armed forces from Iran hostilities

top_stances.summary: Expand hate crime protections and increase identity theft penalties; oppose separate military veteran hate crime category.
top_stances.source_excerpt: supports expanding hate crime protections and increasing penalties for identity theft, while advocating against making military veterans a separate category of hate crime victims

top_stances.summary: Support emergency economic interventions and consumer protection; oppose payday lending regulation delays.
top_stances.source_excerpt: voted in favor of the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act in 2008 during the financial crisis, but opposed CFPB payday lending regulation delays
